channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Southwest Airlines cutting hundreds of jobs
item.description: Southwest Airlines announced Monday that it will cut 1,750 jobs in late April in what will be the first round of layoffs in the company’s 53-year history.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: US Catholic bishops sue Trump administration for halt in funding for refugee settlement
item.description: Catholic bishops are suing the Trump administration over its abrupt halt to funding of refugee resettlement, calling the action unlawful and harmful to newly arrived refugees and to the nation’s largest private resettlement program.
item.dc:creator: Peter Smith, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's firing of 1,000 national park workers raises concerns about maintenance and operating hours
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees who maintain and clean parks, educate visitors and perform other functions as part of its broad-based effort to downsize government.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Questions emerge about what may have caused Delta plane to burst into flames and flip over
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Investigators will consider the weather conditions, as well as the possibility of human error or an aircraft malfunction as they try to determine why a Delta Air Lines jet burst into flames and flipped upside down as it tried to land in Toronto, aviation experts said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: 3 victims killed in Green River Tunnel crash identified, 18 people treated for injuries
item.description: The three people who died in a fiery crash in Wyoming's Green River Tunnel on Friday, Feb. 14 have been identified by officials.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Sheriff says 3 kids found 'basically abandoned' for years at suburban Detroit home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donations of clothes and money are pouring in for three children who were “basically abandoned” by their mother for years, living alone in a suburban Detroit home among trash and feces, a sheriff said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southwest cutting nearly 1,800 jobs, airline says
item.description: Southwest Airlines plans to lay off more than 1,700 employees in a cost-cutting move the company’s CEO described as “unprecedented” in the airline’s 53-year history, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter dies decades after shooting
item.description: Anne Marie Hochhalter, a survivor of the Columbine High School mass shooting, died Sunday, her former principal, Frank DeAngelis, told Nexstar's KDVR on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delphi murders: Gull rules against defense motions seeking to reexamine Richard Allen's conviction
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. -- Special Judge Fran Gull denied recent motions from Richard Allen’s attorneys seeking to reexamine his conviction last year in the Delphi murders case. In an order dated Feb. 14, Gull denied the defense’s Verified Motion to Correct Error (filed on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: How better water systems can help a city survive the next firestorm
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Colorado (AP) — Santa Rosa. Paradise. Boulder County. Lahaina. Los Angeles. All are places that have shown that American cities and their water systems weren't built to withstand wildfire, experts say. Hydrants trickled. Pumps and treatment plants lost power.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: Native American activist Leonard Peltier has been released from a Florida prison, weeks after then-President Joe Biden angered law enforcement officials by commuting his life sentence to home confinement in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Person hospitalized after being thrown into Pennsylvania trash truck
item.description: The fire department says upon arrival, authorities determined that an individual had been inside a dumpster when it was emptied into the truck.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, Fox 5

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mother arrested after leaving 3 children to live alone for years
item.description: Oakland County Sheriff’s Office deputies arrested a 34-year-old Pontiac mother on Friday after she abandoned her three children for years, leaving them in horrendous living conditions.
item.dc:creator: Trevor King, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: “Our adversaries are upping their game every day,” said former Department of Homeland Security cyber chief Suzanne Spaulding. “I’m worried that we are, at the same time, tearing down our defenses.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: A plane that had gone missing Friday night was found early Saturday morning in a remote, wooded area in north Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tours Boeing plane to highlight aircraft maker's delay in delivering a new Air Force One
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump toured a Boeing airplane to check out new hardware and technology features and highlight the aircraft maker's delay in delivering updated versions of the Air Force One presidential aircraft, the White House said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: First homes and weddings have never been more expensive
item.description: Weddings and homeownership are two major goals for many couples, but affording both takes years of careful saving and planning.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Getler, WGN 9

item.title: Trump Media says it lost more than $400 million last year
item.description: The parent company of President Donald Trump’s social networking site Truth Social says it lost $400.9 million last year and its annual revenue declined 12% to $3.6 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: COLUMBIA, Mo. (AP) — Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WGN 9

item.title: US hits international court's top prosecutor with sanctions after Trump's order
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.S. sanctioned the International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor Thursday, following up on President Donald Trump’s order last week targeting the court over its investigations of Israel.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
